diagrams.title: Swing Painting Hierarchy Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the complete flow of Swing's paint() method and its component methods
elements: paint(Graphics g) - main method called by RepaintManager
paintComponent(Graphics g) - where custom painting occurs
Graphics context passed through each method
Override paintComponent() for custom drawing only
diagrams.purpose: Students learn the correct override point (paintComponent) and understand the painting chain responsibility. This is critical for avoiding common mistakes like overriding paint() instead of paintComponent() which breaks UI rendering.

diagrams.title: Repaint Mechanism and Update Cycle
diagrams.description: Illustrates the sequence from user action to screen update in Swing
elements: User Event (mouse click, key press, resize)
paint(Graphics g) called by AWT threading
diagrams.purpose: Students understand the asynchronous nature of Swing painting and why calling repaint() is preferred over directly calling paint(). Learn about the RepaintManager's role in optimizing paint requests.

diagrams.title: Graphics2D Rendering Pipeline
diagrams.description: Shows the Graphics2D class capabilities and rendering options
diagrams.purpose: Students learn about the advanced 2D rendering capabilities beyond basic Graphics methods. Understanding Graphics2D allows for professional-quality custom UI components with gradients, transparency, and transformations.

diagrams.title: Custom Component Painting Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Complete lifecycle of creating and using a custom painted component
elements: Cast Graphics to Graphics2D for advanced features
Use Graphics methods (drawLine, drawRect, fillOval, drawImage, drawString)
Call super.paintComponent(g) for background if needed
diagrams.purpose: Students gain a step-by-step understanding of how to create custom painted components. This serves as a practical guide for implementing charts, games, and custom visualizations.

charts.title: paint() vs paintComponent() vs paintBorder() vs paintChildren()
charts.content: paint() - Entire component painting, calls paintComponent, paintBorder, paintChildren; paintComponent() - Custom drawing area, background clearing; paintBorder() - Border rendering (if set); paintChildren() - Recursive child painting. Rule: Override ONLY paintComponent() for custom drawing, never override paint() unless replacing entire painting chain.

charts.title: Graphics Class Methods Classification
charts.content: Shape Drawing: drawLine, drawRect, drawRoundRect, drawOval, drawArc, drawPolygon, drawString. Shape Filling: fillRect, fillRoundRect, fillOval, fillArc, fillPolygon. Image Rendering: drawImage (all variants). Text Rendering: drawString, drawChars. Clip/Transform: setClip, translate, rotate, scale, shear. State Management: setColor, setFont, setPaint, setStroke, setComposite.

charts.title: Custom Painting Decision Flow
charts.content: Start: Need custom graphics? → Yes → Extend JComponent/JPanel → Override paintComponent(Graphics g) → Cast to Graphics2D for advanced features → Call super.paintComponent(g) first (if opaque) → Add drawing code → Call repaint() when data changes → End. No: Use standard Swing components → End.

charts.title: AWT vs Swing Painting Differences
charts.content: AWT: Heavyweight components (native peer), paint() only, direct Graphics calls, limited customization. Swing: Lightweight components (no native peer), paintComponent() override, Graphics2D advanced features, Pluggable Look-and-Feel, Automatic double buffering, ComponentUI delegate for painting.

branches: Paint Method Hierarchy (paint → paintComponent → paintBorder → paintChildren)
Double Buffering (automatic in Swing, eliminates flicker)
mindMaps.purpose: Provides comprehensive overview connecting all painting concepts. Students see how Swing's architecture separates concerns (UI delegate, component, container) and how the painting pipeline flows from high-level repaint request to actual screen rendering.
